meta_title: For Founders: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Client Workflow to Solve Project Stagnation
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn an advanced client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to maintain momentum and ship revenue-generating SaaS products.
content.answer_block: To stop abandoning projects, you must shift from 'coding' to 'product engineering.' Implement a rigid client-facing workflow that forces incremental delivery. By using Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative feature implementation, you transform abstract ideas into shippable, monetizable milestones before motivation wanes.
content.intro:
The graveyard of abandoned projects is littered with the corpses of 'perfect' architectures and unshipped features. You start with a vision, code for three weeks, and then the complexity wall hits. The excitement evaporates, the bugs multiply, and the project becomes another tab in your browser you're too ashamed to close.

But the failure isn't technical; it's structural. You’re building in a vacuum, without the forcing function of client feedback. By adopting a strict client-centric workflow, you turn the chaotic process of software development into a predictable, high-velocity engine that forces you to deliver value, not just lines of code.

steps.title: Define the Minimum Viable Contract
steps.description: Stop building features. Build a contract. Define one specific outcome a client will pay for and document it as the only acceptable MVP scope.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this project idea: [IDEA]. Generate a 3-page scope document that focuses strictly on one core revenue-generating feature. Remove all 'nice-to-have' technical debt.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project README.md that outlines the MVP scope defined by the Antigravity document.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Flow
steps.description: Before writing logic, define how the client data moves. This prevents the 'refactoring trap' that kills momentum.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a schema for a SaaS MVP handling [CORE_FUNCTION]. Map out the database entities and the primary API endpoints required for the MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the database migration files and Prisma schema based on the Antigravity architecture output.'

steps.title: Implement the 'Client-First' UI Shell
steps.description: Build the interface before the backend. This gives you a tangible asset to 'show' a potential client, creating immediate accountability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-fidelity component structure for a dashboard that displays [CORE_METRIC]. Focus on state management for user input.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the React components for the dashboard shell and integrate the mock data providers.'

steps.title: Automate the Feedback Loop
steps.description: Integrate a system where Claude Code creates a 'Demo Build' every time you finish a feature, forcing you to test it as a user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a CI/CD script that triggers an automated deployment to Vercel/Netlify upon every successful PR merge.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure a GitHub Action to automate staging deployments for client review.'

steps.title: The 48-Hour Delivery Sprint
steps.description: Force a hard deadline. Use Claude Code to execute the final polish of the MVP within 48 hours, treating the codebase like a product release.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a task breakdown for the final 48 hours of development to ensure the core MVP flow is fully functional and error-free.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute the remaining tasks in the breakdown, prioritizing bug fixes and core user flow completion.'

pro_tips: Never start a new feature until the current one is deployed to a live staging URL.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests first; if the tests pass, your feature is 'done' and you cannot touch the code again.
Treat your codebase like a commercial product: if a feature doesn't directly contribute to the client's goal, delete it.
Maintain a 'Changelog' file in your repo; it forces you to acknowledge progress and keeps you motivated.
Use Antigravity to audit your own code every Friday—ask it to find 'over-engineering' and strip it away.
content.next_steps: Once the MVP is live, identify your first three 'beta clients' and give them access. Use their feedback to fuel your next sprint rather than your own assumptions.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping for real results. Every day spent without a client interaction is a day wasted on technical vanity. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
